framework.gpu_acceleration: CuPy or PyTorch tensors for signature computation
framework.reasoning: Paper uses SciPy L-BFGS-B, GPU-vectorized signatures (Peter Foster's code), and NumPy-style array ops. PyTorch used for GPU tensor ops and autograd-free Monte Carlo. signatory library provides fast truncated signature computation on GPU.

tensor_flows.name: Offline Signature Precomputation
pseudocode: W, B ~ Gaussian increments → shape [nMC, T_steps]
sig_N3 = compute_sig(X_aug, N=3) → [nMC, T_steps, 15]  (at each time step)
sig_N7 = compute_sig(X_aug, N=7) → [nMC, 255]  (at terminal T only, for Q)

tensor_flows.name: Online MC Pricing (given ℓ)
pseudocode: per path j: S_T_j = S0 * exp(-‖U_j @ l‖² + l @ stoch_int_j)
IV(K,T,l) = BS_implied_vol(C(K,T,l), S0, K, T, r)

tensor_flows.name: Calibration Optimization Loop
pseudocode: C_model[i] = price_call(l, U, stoch_int, Ki, Ti)  for all 20 contracts
l ← L-BFGS-B update (gradient via finite differences or autodiff)

model.primary_process: heston_variance   # options: heston_variance | fbm_raw | fbm_exp | fbm_shifted_exp

calibration.box_bounds: [-10.0, 10.0]   # ASSUMED: paper mentions box constraints but not bounds

requirements.txt: signatory==1.2.7.1.9.0   # GPU-accelerated signatures; OR use iisignature>=0.24 for CPU
numba>=0.57.0   # optional JIT for fBM simulation
dependencies.notes: signatory requires PyTorch; for CPU-only use iisignature instead. For fBM, Cholesky method is O(T²) — consider FFT-based circulant embedding for large T_steps.

cli_args: --config  PATH         Path to YAML config file

entrypoints.description: Run the analytical Heston ASV expansion calibration (Section 2.1)
Run the VIX-based rough Bergomi calibration (Section 2.2)

risk_assessment.description: Q matrix assembly requires shuffle products up to level 2N+1=7, spanning a 255-dimensional tensor space. Computing all shuffle combinations naively is combinatorially expensive.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Precompute a lookup table mapping (I,J) multi-index pairs to their shuffle expansion coefficients. Cache this table at startup. Use vectorized NumPy ops over nMC paths.

risk_assessment.description: signatory library has strict PyTorch version compatibility constraints and may not install cleanly on all CUDA versions.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Provide fallback to iisignature (CPU-only) or a custom batched signature implementation using torch.einsum.

risk_assessment.description: fBM simulation via naive Volterra Riemann sum is O(T²) per path — infeasible at nMC=800k and T_steps≥100.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use the Hybrid scheme (Bennedsen et al. 2017) for market price generation. For primary process, use Cholesky decomposition of the covariance matrix once (amortized), then sample via matrix-vector products. Reference: McCrickerd & Pakkanen 2018.

risk_assessment.description: X0 interpretation ambiguity: paper states X0=0.1 for Heston variance SDE but does not clarify if this is initial variance or initial vol.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement config flag `x0_is_variance: true/false`; default to X0=0.1 as variance. Expose as a clearly labeled config parameter.

risk_assessment.description: Box constraint bounds for L-BFGS-B not specified in paper — wrong bounds could prevent convergence.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default to [-10,10]; implement as configurable. Run sensitivity test with [-5,5] and [-20,20]. Log final ℓ* to check if bounds are active.

risk_assessment.description: VIX ATMI used in Step 2 of rough Bergomi calibration requires European option prices on the VIX index — synthetic generation requires nested simulation.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement VIX simulation using its definition: VIX_T = sqrt(E_T[∫_{T}^{T+Δ} σ²_s ds] / Δ). Use a second Monte Carlo simulation for E[VIX_T²] to compute the VIX option price.

risk_assessment.description: Cholesky decomposition of -Q(T) may fail if -Q(T) is not numerically positive definite (due to MC noise in signature estimates).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Add small diagonal regularization: -Q + ε*I before Cholesky, with ε=1e-8. Log failure count.
